title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Behavior Case: Bfs Layer Invariant
summary: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bfs layer invariant increment in deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-bfs-shortest-path-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_bfs_shortest_path_5(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Layer Invariant model · Theory Bfs Layer Invariant, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Bfs Layer Invariant precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bfs Layer Invariant model · Theory Bfs Layer Invariant, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8618 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
